geopolitics_summary: Iran war disrupts Middle East energy supplies; EU softens climate rules under US pressure; Russia sanctions escalate.

themes.headline: Italy pursues Libyan gas to offset Iran war supply disruptions
themes.why_it_matters: Iran war has triggered EU gas cost spikes. Italy ramping Libyan pipeline supply signals scarcity premium persists; direct TTF upside risk if Libya production lags demand.

themes.headline: EU weakens methane enforcement rules; oil companies gain exemption scope
themes.why_it_matters: Looser methane rules reduce compliance cost for gas producers, potentially supporting supply availability and moderating gas prices; indirect EUA pressure via lower abatement incentives.

themes.headline: Putin's shadow fleet faces fresh EU sanctions; banking and military targets
themes.why_it_matters: Escalated sanctions on Russian oil/gas transport infrastructure risk further curtailment of EU supply flows, supporting TTF and power curve premium via fuel-switch hedging.

themes.headline: Portugal halves fossil fuel targets over decade post-Iran war cost surge
themes.why_it_matters: Accelerated EU decarbonization policy response to energy price shock; increased renewable deployment reduces long-term fossil fuel baseload, supporting green power and EUA floor pricing.

themes.headline: Wind power faces anti-renewable campaigns mirroring US political pressure in EU
themes.why_it_matters: Political opposition to wind delays deployment and grid integration, reducing renewable baseload; supports fossil-fuel and power-curve upside if renewables capacity growth stalls.

watchlist: EU methane enforcement guidelines final implementation date & exemption scope clarification.
Libya gas production ramp-up timeline vs. Italy import target (supply/demand mismatch risk).
Further Russia sanctions announcements; shadow fleet/banking channel closures impact LNG & pipeline flows.
Portuguese fossil fuel phase-out regulatory roadmap; renewable capacity auction schedules 2026–2027.
